what_it_does: Builds a reverse index (the code:// symbol registry) from every code: reference in register.json and addresses.json, resolves each against the local company source tree by reusing refcheck, and emits code-symbols.json mapping each referenced symbol to a stable id, its file, kind, resolution status, and the corpus owners that reference it. Optionally adds semantic nearest-neighbor edges via the existing nodes/embed and nodes/retrieve nodes, degrading with a warning if the embedding endpoint is unavailable.
observations: Reuses refcheck primitives (collect_refs, split_refs, parse_ref, _resolve, _all_defs, _nearest_def) instead of reimplementing forward resolution.
Handles multiple ref forms: path:symbol, path:line, bare path, /api/... route literals, and Suite.verb method references.
Globs and directories (nodes/*.py, voice/) are explicitly reported as not_indexable rather than silently dropped.
Stable ids use the resolved file stem so bare and qualified refs collapse to the same symbol entry.
Reverse merge collapses multiple refs to the same id and collects de-duplicated referenced_by owners.
Tracks id collisions when the same stem resolves to different files in different contexts.
Classifies symbol kind from the declaration keyword (def, class, function, const, let, var).
X11 semantic edge embeds representative_text (symbol + kind + stem + owner labels) and ranks other entries by cosine via nodes/retrieve.
On embed failure the semantic field is omitted entirely with a loud warning; the structural index remains intact.
Includes a self-validation step (_validate) before the emitted registry is trusted.
run_corpus reads register.json and addresses.json and writes code-symbols.json with a printed summary.
standouts: Reverse-index complement to refcheck: shared symbols and unresolved references fall out naturally from the merge.
Explicit degrade-with-warning behavior for the optional semantic edge when the BGE-M3 endpoint is down.
Honest accounting of non-symbol ref forms (globs/directories) in the summary instead of silent dropping.
conventions: Injectable seams (resolve, embed_fn, bridge_lines, warn) for hermetic tests and graceful degradation.
Reuse existing nodes (embed/retrieve) rather than reimplementing embeddings or cosine ranking.
Loud failure and validation: malformed registry raises, id collisions and unresolved symbols are surfaced.
Stable identifiers derived from resolved file stem to unify differently-qualified refs.
Kind classification derived from the declaration line keyword.
concerns: symbol_id uses only the file stem, so files with the same basename in different directories intentionally collide; the collision guard catches conflicts but the id scheme loses path information.
build_registry calls index_ref twice per sub-ref (once for the not_indexable tally and once inside build_index), which is redundant work.
_default_embed mutates sys.path as a side effect when invoked.
For unresolved code files with a :symbol hint, kind is always set to 'def' regardless of whether the missing symbol was a class or const.
The exact input/output contracts of nodes.embed.run and nodes.retrieve.run are not defined in this file.
notes: This is the reverse-map builder for the code:// coordinate branch: it turns every code reference in the design corpus into an addressable symbol entry and optionally layers conceptual nearest-neighbor edges on top.
questions: What exact schema does refcheck.collect_refs return, and what fields are required in register.json and addresses.json?
How does refcheck._resolve locate files under ~/company, and what does it return on success or failure?
Where do the owner label/represents texts come from in the items passed to build_index?
What are the precise input/output shapes of nodes.embed.run and nodes.retrieve.run?
Is the stem-only id collision an intentional unification trade-off or a limitation to be mitigated later?
How are /api/... route literals expected to be represented inside bridge.py?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose (reverse code-symbol registry with drift detection and optional semantic edges) matches what the implementation does.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make every code reference in the design corpus addressable and traceable, surface drift when referenced symbols disappear, and add a conceptual neighborhood on top of the structural call graph without breaking when the embedding fabric is unavailable.
novelty: The explicit degrade-with-warning rule for optional semantic edges, and the honest tally of non-indexable refs rather than silently dropping globs or directories.
contribution: A reverse code-symbol index that lets a fused system ask 'who references this symbol?' and detect unresolved references, plus a pluggable pattern for adding semantic nearest-neighbor edges that fails safely.
summary_for_embedding: symbols.py builds the code:// reverse symbol registry from register.json and addresses.json code references, resolving them against the local source tree via refcheck and emitting code-symbols.json with file, kind, resolution status, and referenced-by owners, optionally adding semantic nearest-neighbor edges using existing embedding and retrieval nodes.
intention_for_embedding: To make code symbols referenced by the design corpus addressable, traceable, and semantically navigable while surfacing drift and degrading safely if the embedding service is unavailable.

symbols.does: Finds the 1-based declaration line of a named symbol in source lines, including forms refcheck misses.
symbols.description: Scans source lines with a regex matching export/default/async declarations of def/class/function/const/let/var followed by the symbol name, returning the line number or None. Owned by symbols.py for precise per-symbol resolution.

symbols.does: Classifies a declaration line into def, class, or const.
symbols.description: Matches the leading keyword of a source line and maps 'class' to class, const/let/var to const, and def/async def/function to def; defaults to def when no recognized keyword is found.

symbols.does: Extracts the stable file stem from a repo-relative path.
symbols.description: Returns the basename without extension so that differently qualified paths such as 'runtime/suite.py' and 'suite.py' collapse to the same stem, enabling stable code:// ids.

symbols.does: Builds the stable code:// identifier for a file and optional symbol.
symbols.description: Returns code://<stem>/<symbol> when a symbol is present and code://<stem> for file-only references, unifying references regardless of how the path was written.

symbols.does: Indexes one sub-reference into a symbol entry or returns None for non-symbol forms.
symbols.description: Handles /api/ routes, Suite.verb forms, path:symbol, path:line, and bare path refs; resolves files via refcheck, determines kind and resolution status, and reports globs or directories as not indexable.

symbols.does: Builds the reverse index collapsing refs that land on the same symbol id.
symbols.description: Splits each raw reference, indexes sub-refs, merges entries by stable id while tracking id collisions and preferring resolving sightings, and collects de-duplicated referenced_by owners; optionally gathers owner label texts into a sidecar.

symbols.does: Assembles the text representation used for semantic embedding of a symbol.
symbols.description: Combines symbol name, kind, file stem, and the labels or represents texts of corpus owners that reference it, giving the embedding context about what the symbol is for.
Lazily imports nodes.embed from the repo root, calls embed.run with the text, and returns the vector; raises on endpoint failure so attach_semantic_edges can degrade.

symbols.does: Adds semantically_nearest[] to each registry entry in place.
symbols.description: Embeds representative_text for every entry, builds a corpus of other entries, ranks them by cosine via nodes.retrieve, and stores top-K ids with scores; skips the field entirely with a loud warning if embedding fails.

symbols.does: Returns ids referenced by two or more corpus owners.
symbols.description: Simple filter over the reverse index to surface symbols whose changes could ripple across multiple features or addresses.
Collects refs, tallies non-indexable forms, builds the structural reverse index, attaches semantic edges, and produces summary statistics plus lists of unresolved, shared, and colliding symbols.

symbols.does: Validates the emitted registry shape before it is trusted.
symbols.description: Checks that symbols is a dict, every id starts with code://, required fields are present, and each entry has at least one referenced_by owner.

symbols.does: CLI entry point that builds and writes code-symbols.json for the real corpus.
symbols.description: Reads register.json and addresses.json, calls build_registry, validates the result, writes it to code-symbols.json, and prints a human-readable summary including drift and shared-symbol reports.
Thin wrapper so the module can be run as a script.
